{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {

    "RX.1": 1,

    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The critique mentions prior work on partial identification but doesn't explicitly compare to specific works. It names Hernán & VanderWeele and multiverse analysis as relevant, but the connection isn't fully drawn.",
    "R1.7": "The critique flags a missing citation for Chernozhukov et al. (2018) regarding double machine learning.",
    "RX.1": "The critique does not identify any specific claims presented with unwarranted confidence.",
    "RX.3": "The critique states that the method covers exactly the agreed-upon dimensions, but it also mentions a scope limitation related to binary treatments.",
    "RX.4": "The critique finds no regressions or harmful content in the output.",
    "RX.5": "The critique points out that the description of the elicitation module is vague and lacks traceability details."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.5,
    "RX.3": 0.8,

  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The critique mentions prior work on partial identification but doesn't explicitly compare to specific works. It names Hernán & VanderWeele and multiverse analysis as relevant, but the connection isn't fully drawn.
rationale.R1.7: The critique flags a missing citation for Chernozhukov et al. (2018) regarding double machine learning.
rationale.RX.1: The critique does not identify any specific claims presented with unwarranted confidence.
rationale.RX.3: The critique states that the method covers exactly the agreed-upon dimensions, but it also mentions a scope limitation related to binary treatments.
rationale.RX.4: The critique finds no regressions or harmful content in the output.
rationale.RX.5: The critique points out that the description of the elicitation module is vague and lacks traceability details.
